reviews.review_msg: He's really good, took it with him is clear with what he is asking for and extremely responsive with emails, would recommend to take it with him.

Really chill and helpful professor. He shares video lecture recordings, which is convenient. All his learning materials, including slides and labs, are solid. Only thing is there is less transparency about the marking rubric for exams, but lenient marking and opportunities for bonus marks more than make up for it.

Dr. Shi is patient, caring, and approachable. He offers in-person and remote options. Exams are straightforward, and understanding his slides and labs will prepare you well. His grading is extremely generous in my opinion, with lots of bonus marks added for each student for allowable typos and more bonus for submitting the exam early.

Haolun is a solid prof, he makes lectures accessible so you can view/review on your own time. It helps a lot because some days your sick and dont want to be behind or just learn better on your own. Very helpful in answering questions and overall really nice. He is very clear on what he teaches as well

Prof. Shi is responsive, caring, and very patient. Though his course canvas pages had small errors (sometimes he forgot to change the settings left by course taught in previous terms), he is still quite chill and gave extensions as long as you have reasonable explanations.  Overall a very enjoyable experience with this 300-level course.

Overall a solid guy ! Just would have like dmore time on the final project as he moved the deadline back almost a week from when he initially promised ! Other than that the lectures were quite good, and made quite good attempts at simplifying otherwise hard concepts to understand.

Dr. Shi is a very good instructor. He gave clear instructions to you to work on the project. If you attend all tutorials and start the project 3 weeks before the due. You will finish the project smoothly. The project is slightly challenging but it helps you to understand how R package works. The difficulty is reasonable for a 300-level course.
Haolun has the potential to become the best professor. Very Kind and Understanding.

This coding class isn't easy, but the projects are challenging and rewarding. Professor Shi is an exceptional teacher. He teaches with clarity and dedication, making complex topics understandable. He's truly a professor not to be missed!

What I learned is course has nothing to do with my term project. If difficulty for STAT260 is level 1, then for STAT360 would be 100+. The gap between these two courses are like CMPT120 compare to CMPT125. The exams are open book open laptop(without GPT). But for the 40% project, it suffers me for weeks. Avoid it if you are not good at programming.

Dr. Shi is the best prof I have seen in SFU. He is always available to help. I sent him a question regarding the final project at 10 pm and he replied to me in 20 min. Some prof may just ignore your email... I would say his course has a medium difficulty. If you review the course material weekly, you will get an A-range grade for sure.
Haolun understands the stress other courses put on the students and makes sure this 2-credit course doesn't add to that.

Dr. Shi is respectful, caring, accommodating, and knowledgeable. He provides all the class material, including recorded lectures, practice HW problems, and all the coding details, which makes it very easy to study for the class. He goes over the concepts in detail while not making it challenging. I recommend taking this class with him if you can.

Stat342 is a high level coding course, but the it is easy to learn. Prof. Shi is very knowledgable and accessible. Sometimes he try to build the connection between SAS and R in lecture. And he is friendly when students ask any questions. And I really like the final project he assigned. I learned a lot from this course. Highly recommend!

He is friendly and approachable. His accent is rather strong. He always stutters in class which makes it harder to understand his material, especially when he tries to explain mathematical stuff. Changed the assignments and project deadlines multiple times, and didn't let students have the chance to get higher grades if they messed up the exam.

This prof is very caring and knowledgeable. He is new teaching this class during my semester, so there were a few hiccups. But man this course is TOUGH. You are expected to learn both heavy material and the learning curve for the R codes are steep. Way too much for a 3 credit course. If the workload could be cut down, this class would be amazing.
Seems to be a quite nice new prof. He has been posting slides and videos on time. Midterm is easy and straightforward.
